questions.question: Who is the central character in the poem who needs help?
questions.correct_answer: An old woman standing at the street crossing
questions.explanation: The poem begins by describing an elderly woman who is weak and afraid to cross the snowy street alone.

questions.question: What season is described in the poem?
questions.explanation: The poet mentions snow and cold weather, showing that the story takes place on a chilly winter day.

questions.question: Why was the old woman unable to cross the street easily?
questions.correct_answer: Because she was old, weak, and the street was slippery with snow
questions.explanation: The poem describes her as slow and trembling, which made crossing the busy street difficult.

questions.question: How did most of the schoolboys behave when they passed the woman?
questions.correct_answer: They ignored her and hurried past
questions.explanation: The boys were busy playing and laughing, so they did not notice the old woman’s difficulty.

questions.question: Who finally helped the old woman?
questions.correct_answer: A young boy from the group of schoolboys
questions.explanation: One boy noticed her difficulty and kindly offered to help her cross the street.

questions.question: What did the boy do to help the woman?
questions.correct_answer: He guided her safely across the street
questions.explanation: The boy supported her trembling steps and ensured she crossed the road without harm.

questions.question: Why did the boy say the woman was ‘somebody’s mother’?
questions.correct_answer: Because he realised she was someone’s loved parent
questions.explanation: The boy thought about how his own mother might need help one day, which motivated him to act kindly.

questions.question: What did the old woman do after reaching home?
questions.correct_answer: She prayed for the boy who helped her
questions.explanation: The woman felt grateful and asked God to bless the boy for his kindness.

questions.question: What message does the poem teach readers?
questions.correct_answer: We should help others and show empathy
questions.explanation: The poem shows that small acts of kindness can make a big difference in someone’s life.

questions.question: Why is the poem titled 'Somebody’s Mother'?
questions.correct_answer: Because the old woman represents someone’s loved mother
questions.explanation: The title reminds readers that every elderly person is someone’s parent and deserves care and respect.

notes.title: Old Woman in the Poem
notes.content: The poem describes an elderly woman who struggles to cross a snowy street because of her age and weakness.

notes.title: Kindness of the Young Boy
notes.content: One boy shows empathy by helping the old woman cross the street safely.

notes.title: The Boy’s Thought
notes.content: The boy helps the woman because he thinks she might be someone’s mother, just like his own.

notes.title: Gratitude of the Woman
notes.content: The old woman expresses her gratitude by praying for the boy’s happiness and success.

notes.title: Message of the Poem
notes.content: The poem teaches that kindness, empathy, and helping others are important human values.

facts.fact: The poem describes a winter scene with snow and a busy street.
Many children passed the old woman without helping her.
One boy showed kindness by helping the woman cross the street.
The poem emphasises empathy and respect for the elderly.
